This is a project that simply introduces Simple Linear Regression and helps understand and predict the relationship between two continuous variables.
'y' is considered the dependent variable and 'x' is considered the independent variable
-We use Libraries such as NumPy, Pandas and scikitlearn to model the relationships between the variables

EXAMPLES
Examples 1:
We learn to import the Python Libraries which we need to work with on our project
We Load our data as a Pandas DataFrame
In this case, Dollar Exchange rate as our depedent variable and the monthly value export as our independent variable.
-For future reference you change the columns names to reflect the notation (y, x)
Use the matplotlib library to create a few plots of the data

Example 2:
Create a little more visually appealing than the two plots in Example 1
In this project, we'll plot both dependent data series on the same graph
Two separate y-axes will be assigned: one for each series
A legend will be displayed near the top of the plot

Example 3:
This is a simple project that entails some parts of example 1 and 2 
Simply in example 3 what is shown is the relationship between the two variables using a scatter plot

Example 4:
The focus on this project is finding the best fit line

Example 5:
The calculated fit of the line is checked by measuring how far the true y-values of each point are from their corresponding y-value the line(errors)
